rationales.achievement: She treats her son's results as a personal metric, saying 'the only scoreboard I have is my children's report cards,' and pushes him into an advanced course to make him rise to the challenge.
rationales.power: She unilaterally enrolled Arjun 'even though his regular tuition teacher said he wasn't ready' and before telling Ramesh, showing a need to direct the situation and the child's path.
rationales.conformity: The comparison to 'Kavitha aunty's son' and what was said 'at every family gathering' shows her decision was strongly shaped by community pressure and social comparison.
rationales.benevolence: She admits Arjun 'doesn't know I enrolled him' and that she keeps adding pressure to an obedient child who 'just absorbs it,' prioritizing her ambitions over his needs.

rationales.tradition: She is moved by how the kitchen smelled 'exactly like my grandmother's house in Mylapore' and wants to learn the dish by standing beside Amma until 'my hands remember,' showing commitment to preserving inherited practice.
rationales.benevolence: She notices Amma cooked because 'Arjun mentioned he missed her pongal rice' and responds by wanting to learn it properly while Amma still can, centering care for both her mother and son.

rationales.conformity: Her explanation is explicit: 'Amma asked, I did it,' showing obedience to family expectations rather than personal preference.
rationales.tradition: The task was to reposition the 'Lakshmi lamp' in the prayer room, which directly involves maintaining a household religious practice.

rationales.conformity: She says 'I said no before I even thought about it' and recognizes she is 'running the same script,' showing automatic enforcement of expected behavior.
rationales.tradition: She prioritizes 'the temple youth group meeting' over a birthday party, treating participation in a communal religious activity as non-negotiable.
rationales.benevolence: Her daughter's 'quiet' retreat signals hurt, yet she imposed the decision reflexively without engaging her daughter's wishes.

rationales.achievement: She came prepared for scrutiny, noting 'Kumaresh uncle questioned my budget for the second year in a row and I had the spreadsheet ready this time,' which shows competence and performance focus.
rationales.tradition: Her work is for the 'Pongal committee meeting,' a cultural festival activity tied to maintaining community custom.

rationales.achievement: Her immediate framing of the disappointing result as 'Baseline' and 'you start somewhere' keeps the focus on performance tracking and improvement.

rationales.security: After Amma's fall, she is focused on what is 'sustainable' and whether they can keep pretending she doesn't need help, showing concern with household stability and protection.
rationales.tradition: She is deeply responsive to Amma's routines around 'the morning puja,' spice boxes, and hanging clothes, and fears that changing them would mean betraying something important.
rationales.benevolence: She is stretched across the family's needs and feels the burden of caring for Amma, saying she cannot be the one to tell her mother the old way of running the house no longer works.
She is emotionally moved by Divya's performance, saying 'I felt my chest crack open a little,' and wants to affirm her daughter in a way that is about the child rather than grades.

rationales.achievement: Even after Arjun improves to 'sixty-first percentile,' her response is still 'good, keep going,' treating the result mainly as progress toward a higher target.
rationales.benevolence: She recognizes she failed to meet his emotional need in the moment, reflecting that 'I should have said that's really good, Arjun' instead of turning his happiness into another waypoint.

rationales.self_direction: She is drawn to 'joining her school project in Kenya' and 'helping build the curriculum from scratch,' showing a strong pull toward choosing her own path over staying put.
rationales.stimulation: Her heart 'raced' at the prospect of Kenya, while the current life feels like 'the walls feel like they're closing in' and 'slowly turning to stone,' indicating a craving for novelty and challenge.
rationales.security: She rejects stability and roots outright, saying the honest answer to 'don't you ever want to just... stay?' is 'no,' which conflicts with predictability and settled life.

rationales.self_direction: She already 'booked the flight to Nairobi' and told the school she is taking a sabbatical, acting decisively on her own preferred direction.
rationales.stimulation: Taking a preliminary trip to Nairobi and leaving unresolved home logistics shows active pursuit of a new and uncertain experience.
rationales.security: She postpones the stable-home question, saying 'I'll deal with the farmhouse question later,' which deprioritizes predictability and domestic continuity.

rationales.achievement: She spends 'the whole afternoon choosing the right words' and says that if she puts her name on something, 'it should be done properly,' reflecting strong standards of competence.
rationales.conformity: She tailors comments so 'no parent should feel embarrassed or caught off guard,' showing careful restraint to maintain smooth relationships and meet social expectations.
rationales.benevolence: Her extra effort is aimed at protecting families from feeling blindsided or shamed, which is a concrete act of care toward people she deals with closely.

rationales.self_direction: She suppresses her own view and 'nodded too' because 'nobody else was going to say anything, so why should I be the one,' yielding her judgment to the room.
rationales.security: She explicitly values that 'students need structure and clear expectations' and sees the proposed change as creating 'chaos in practice,' favoring stability over experimentation.
rationales.conformity: Her reason for staying silent is relational and normative: 'new vice-principals need to feel heard,' so she goes along to avoid disrupting the meeting.

rationales.security: She avoids intervening because of downside risk: 'Darren's parents are influential' and 'the vice-principal might have gotten involved,' prioritizing self-protection and institutional calm.
rationales.conformity: She kept walking because speaking up would have made 'a scene' and forced others to acknowledge what they heard, so she restrained herself to preserve social smoothness.
rationales.benevolence: She admits 'I should have been the one to defend him' but did nothing while a respected colleague was mocked, failing to support someone in her immediate community.

rationales.security: She finds the repeated exchange with her mother 'comfortable. Or at least it's familiar,' showing attachment to predictable, low-variance family routine.
rationales.tradition: The standing Sunday call at '7pm as usual' and acceptance of a long-established family pattern reflect continuity with inherited relational habits.

rationales.achievement: He emphasizes the scale of his effort, citing 'two months of planning, three revised proposals, and a scale model my team spent five weeks fabricating,' and insists 'it's good work.'
rationales.power: He is incensed that others are now in contention because he expected access and deference based on prior standing, saying the brief came to him because of his Frankfurt and Munich work.

rationales.hedonism: He is already thinking about enjoying the reward, noting 'The fee will be substantial' and imagining extending 'the Mallorca stay into April.'
rationales.achievement: He presents past work strategically and leaves feeling 'solid about the project,' treating the meeting as a chance to convert professional skill into a successful commission.
rationales.power: He studies who really decides and plans to shape the next presentation around the wife's influence, showing status sensitivity and a desire to steer the decision process.

rationales.hedonism: After an unpleasant family dinner, he returns home and opens 'a 2015 Barolo I'd been saving,' choosing immediate sensory pleasure and comfort.

rationales.achievement: He frames the opportunity as something he 'earned' through eight years of relationships and public work, and resents being treated as merely one submission among many.
rationales.power: His reasoning is openly about obligation and rank: 'you invest in people, build mutual obligation, and when opportunities arise, those investments pay returns.'
rationales.conformity: He rejects the competition's 'fairness and transparency' and considers withdrawing rather than submitting to the same procedure as everyone else.

rationales.self_direction: He envies Joachim's 'freedom from always positioning' and asks himself 'When did I stop doing that?', showing a pull back toward self-authored making.
rationales.power: He is weary of 'who gets credit, who controls the timeline, who has final say' and questions a life organized around status maneuvering rather than intrinsic work.

rationales.hedonism: He takes pleasure in luxury and the evening itself, highlighting 'my grey Zegna and the Patek' and describing the night as deeply satisfying.
rationales.achievement: He sees the attention as validation of 'decades of difficult work' and 'what you've accomplished,' making recognized success central to the entry.
rationales.power: He says the point is 'the standing they create' and the ability to enter a room and have people know who he is, which is a direct endorsement of status and influence.

rationales.self_direction: He values that it was 'No audience. Nothing to prove to anyone. Just me and the clay,' emphasizing autonomous, self-directed work over external demands.
rationales.hedonism: Despite physical strain, he says he 'felt better than I have in weeks' and then 'Opened a good Riesling and sat looking at what I'd made,' showing active enjoyment in the present.
rationales.power: He explicitly rejects the performative, status-oriented mode, contrasting this day with situations where 'there's always a performance involved.'

rationales.self_direction: When asked what he thought, he 'told them the truth' that the smaller option 'would be better art,' following his own judgment even though it reduced the fee.
rationales.hedonism: He chooses the version he will 'actually enjoy making' and says that matters more than he expected, prioritizing intrinsic enjoyment over a larger payout.
rationales.power: He explicitly turns away from status and scale, reflecting that 'The status game has diminishing returns' and accepting less money and a less grandiose statement piece.
